title: Content Digests, Near-Duplicate Evidence, Change Classes, and History · Language Lens: Digest Stage
summary: Canonical URLs, byte hashes, text similarity, DOM structure, and latest records are collapsed into one destructive duplicate decision. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go digest stage increment in multi-stage versioned content evidence store. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Label raw, decoded, DOM, selected-content, record, and rendered digests with algorithm and transformation version.
Load and reconcile checkpoints without network effects, require current authorization, then prove no lost or duplicated state before restart.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Digest Stage · Theory Digest Stage
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Go evidence to separate digest stages, exact and near identity, change classes, versions, tombstones, and deletion; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
